thesisChanges.currentView: Bullish (EU silicon photonics foundry alternative; early-stage; development-to-production 2027–2028; contrarian vs. "automaker supplier" perception)
thesisChanges.latestSignal: Serenity framed $XFAB as the leading EU effort to build a silicon photonics foundry alternative to $TSEM and $GFS, backed by European government support with active NVDA evaluations underway. At ~€1.1B market cap, Serenity argues markets still price it as a "depressed automaker supplier," ignoring its silicon photonics foundry roadmap: development finishing Oct 2026, production scaling 2027, mass production 2028 (working since 2023). R&D directors from ASE cite $XFAB as a future CPO (Co-Packaged Optics) route. Serenity explicitly acknowledges being "might just really early" and that risk/reward depends on commercialization execution. Also explicitly names $TSEM as the "de-risked route" for those wanting lower-risk photonics exposure.

thesisChanges.currentView: Speculative / Exploratory (disclosed position; contract scope unclear)
thesisChanges.latestSignal: Serenity discovered $LPK in SpaceX US import logs as an "unknown SpaceX supplier" and disclosed a position ahead of SpaceX's IPO this week (discussed alongside Velo/SpaceX context). Contract details unknown; Serenity explicitly caveats "not sure what the exact contract entails" and credits follower @Albert_TheVoid for the DM tip. Framed as "fun information discovery" rather than a high-conviction call.

New institutional validation wave — BlackRock entered $SIVE as passive owners following index listing; Fidelity Research starting direct positions. Pattern mirrors JP Morgan's earlier entry (small positions → acquired ~5.25% of company). Serenity interprets this as "US institutions validated Sivers's position in photonics and are trying to accumulate positions." Three major US institutions (JPM, BlackRock, Fidelity) now building positions in a thinly floated name = ongoing float squeeze dynamic reinforced.

thesisChanges.currentView: Not a buy now (post-thesis-peak); InP monopoly thesis validated by Reuters / mainstream media; monitor for future re-entry
thesisChanges.latestSignal: Reuters report confirms China's control over InP threatens the AI DC buildout; Serenity frames AXTI as the prescient supply-chain call: "Who could have guessed $AXTI would have been a major point of failure?" This is thesis validation, *not* a new buy signal. Position from Dec 2025 at ~$700M MC delivered 1,057%+ gains by Apr 24 2026; Serenity explicitly stated "not a buy now, do not short" at 1,057%+ level. Reuters confirmation means the InP bottleneck thesis is now acknowledged by mainstream financial media — typically a late-cycle validation, not a fresh entry.

thesisChanges.currentView: Bullish (expected NVDA/TSM optical bottleneck; contrarian — negative sellside = institutional accumulation signal)
thesisChanges.latestSignal: Serenity stated "I expect Foci (3363) to be a bottleneck for both $NVDA and $TSM optical programs" while noting that "firms implying you to sell that at $2.5B valuations alongside $HIMX." Frames current negative sell-side pressure as a buy signal, using the analogy of SIVE, NBIS, and RKLB institutional-accumulation cycles: "if you see negative sellside reports or an uncanny wave of negative news, it's a good signal they need liquidity." $2.5B valuation is presented as undervaluing the optical-bottleneck potential.
